text: There's been a number of strange
occurrences over on this wonderful
platform YouTube.com. For example,
starting on February 16th, we had this
tweet from Pirate Nation saying that
YouTube is escalating its crackdown on
ad blockers causing a number of issues
for the site, such as users reporting
that comment sections appear to be
turned off and video descriptions are
missing entirely. And one day after that
news, YouTube apparently had this major
outage that many users reported
experiencing. Some reporting that
everything was working fine on YouTube
except their actual homepage. Others
using YouTube TV saying that they were
logged out of their accounts and unable
to log back in for a while with an
official response from team YouTube
about all this saying if you're having
trouble accessing YouTube right now,
you're not alone. Our teams are looking
into this and we'll follow up here with
updates. Update: an issue with our
recommendation system prevented videos
from appearing across services on
YouTube, including the homepage, the
app, YouTube music, YouTube Kids. The
homepage is back, but we're still
working on a full fix. More coming soon.
I've had my own issue here on YouTube
with my timestamps. Actually, I had a
lovely short chat this morning in the
comment section with a viewer who was
asking if I could put timestamps on a
video. And I mentioned that I actually
did and you can see in the description
that they're there, but for some reason
for a number of weeks now, I'll add
timestamps and they'll be fine. and then
like I'll check on the video hours later
or the next day and they're still there
in the description, but they stopped
actually working in the videos
themselves. It is pretty annoying that I
put the timestamps there to help people
and then they just stop working
randomly. Not to rant about it too much,
but yeah, it's a shame. Seems like a lot
going on all at once and I'm sure I
didn't even mention everything, but
that's some of what's been going on. Let
me know what you think about the latest
and if you've experienced any of these
issues yourself. Thank you for tuning in
and I'll see you in the next
